description_long: This is a short section of a wooden picket fence, featuring five vertical pickets with pointed tops and two horizontal rails connecting them. The pickets are evenly spaced, and the rails are slightly misaligned, giving the fence a rustic, weathered appearance. The wood surface shows a faded, light brown color with visible grain and some signs of aging or wear. The fence is relatively thin when viewed from the side, indicating it is a lightweight, standalone segment rather than a full enclosure. There are no visible decorative elements or embellishments beyond the pointed tops of the pickets. This type of fence is commonly found in residential yards, gardens, or parks to demarcate boundaries or provide a visual barrier.
description: A rustic, weathered wooden picket fence segment with five pointed pickets and two horizontal rails, showing a faded light brown color and simple construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing five pointed pickets and two horizontal rails, with visible wood grain and slight misalignment.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the thinness of the fence and the alignment of the pickets and rails.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the same five pickets and two rails, with less visible texture detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again highlighting the fence's thin structure and the arrangement of the rails.
